title: Controlled External Technical Review Handoff Pack
decision_rationale: GL-222 answers all ten required handoff questions. It documents the current allowed review posture, eligible and prohibited materials, allowed and prohibited claims, known limitations, known false-positive classes, reviewer-safe reproduction commands, security-sensitive report routing, the public/export/publish approval boundary, and the next roadmap. All NO-GO decisions from GL-221 remain unchanged. No backend source, migrations, schema, dependencies, GitHub workflows, snapshot publish scripts, or production deployment config are modified. No public export is created. No reviewer is contacted.

current_controlled_external_review_postu.posture: Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries

handoff_package_purpose: Internal documentation answering the ten required questions for a future controlled external technical review. Does not initiate reviewer outreach, create a public export, publish to public GitHub, or change visibility.
reviewer_scope: Auth baseline: fail-closed startup, operator token hashing, tenant binding, challenge-response, token expiry and rotation model
Audit log baseline: append-only hash-chain, tamper-evidence, immutability triggers, hash-chain verification helper
Tenant isolation baseline: server-derived tenant context, application-layer tenant-scoped routing, absence of cross-tenant leakage in test suite
API contract: docs/openapi.yaml endpoints, request/response shapes, error semantics
Grant lifecycle: create/approve/deny/revoke flows with synthetic/demo data
Rate-limit baseline: request rate limiting, structured logging, correlation ID
Runtime hardening: JSON body enforcement, query parameter validation, CORS origin hardening, private key file permissions
Identity/access posture: static admin token baseline, absence of production OAuth/OIDC/JWT, identity gap documentation (GL-219)
Infrastructure posture: ephemeral/local-only SQLite and optional PostgreSQL, dry-run backup/restore, no production infrastructure (GL-220)
Workspace posture: workspace_id reserved/nullable, not production-enforced, gap documentation (GL-221)
Test suite: full backend test suite structure, known false-positive classes, absence of real regressions
Verification scripts: local-only dry-run/plan gate scripts, grant lifecycle evidence bundle, first verifiable output helper
excluded_scope: Real customer data, real grant applications, real institutional data
Real credentials, tokens, DSNs, private keys, auth headers
Internal Forgejo URLs, private IP addresses, private hostnames, private filesystem paths
Internal CI/CD configuration referencing private services
website-design/ directory and website-design import/report files
setup.py, SDK pyproject.toml, package.json, package-lock.json, package publishing metadata
Production deployment configuration (Kubernetes, Terraform, Helm, cloud provider)
TLS certificates or private key files
GitHub workflow files without separate review gate
Snapshot publish scripts without separate review and approval
Public release branches, public release tags, public export directories
Database dumps, backup files, operational logs with real data
eligible_review_materials: Reviewed docs/ artifacts for resolved issues (no internal-infrastructure references)
backend/src/ reviewed source files (no real secrets, subject to per-file scan)
backend/tests/ (synthetic/demo data only, known false-positive classes disclosed)
prohibited_materials: .env* files with real credentials
Raw PostgreSQL DSNs with real hostnames or passwords
Raw tokens, auth headers, or Bearer tokens
PEM private key blocks (RSA, OPENSSH, EC, DSA)
Real admin tokens or operator tokens
Real SMTP, S3, or cloud credentials
Real customer data, real grant applications, real institutional data, real PII
Real private grant data or private attachments
Database dumps, backup files, or logs containing real data
Internal Forgejo URLs, private IPs, private hostnames, internal filesystem paths
website-design/ directory and all contents
docs/website_design_workspace_import_report.md and similarly named files
setup.py, SDK pyproject.toml, package.json, package-lock.json
Analytics/tracking integrations or data collection forms
.github/workflows/ files without separate review
Snapshot publish scripts unless separately reviewed
Production deployment config (Kubernetes, Terraform, Helm, cloud)
Release metadata, public release branches, public release tags
Data directories with real operational data
.git/, caches, venvs, __pycache__/
allowed_claims: GrantLayer is Developer Preview / Controlled Preview with strict boundaries
Local evaluation and controlled pilot with synthetic/demo data only
Tenant/workspace isolation: baseline implemented, not production-complete
Ephemeral live PostgreSQL validation passed with synthetic/demo data (GL-206B)
Fail-closed auth baseline implemented; not production-grade IAM
Operator token hashing and tenant binding implemented
Structured logging and rate-limit baseline implemented
Controlled external technical review allowed only with strict boundaries and synthetic/demo data
All examples use synthetic identifiers only
All tokens and keys in examples are placeholders
Security-sensitive reports route to GitHub Security Advisories
Production SaaS is not claimed and not supported
Real customer/private grant/institutional data is not ready
workspace_id is reserved/nullable and not production-enforced
Identity/access posture: static admin token baseline; no OIDC/SAML/SSO/MFA
Public snapshot/export requires a later explicit approval gate
prohibited_claims: Production SaaS ready / production-grade / enterprise ready
Ready for real customer data / private grant data / institutional data
Compliance certification achieved (GDPR, SOC2, ISO, or any other)
Official SDK available / official package published
Independent security audit completed / penetration test passed
Multi-tenant production isolation guaranteed / database RLS enforced
Public website is a production marketing launch
Any claim implying a support SLA or uptime guarantee
Any claim that implies real customer deployments are running

real_customer_private_grant_institutiona.rationale: No production-grade security controls, no data governance/legal approval, no compliance certification
requirements_before_change: Separate data governance and legal approval
Compliance certification relevant to jurisdiction and data type (not yet achieved)

requirements_before_any_export: All GL-218 safety scan checklist items pass
Per-file secret scanning and claim-safety scanning of export candidate
Explicit owner approval for the specific export candidate
No forbidden files in the export candidate
Manual human review before any push

identity_access_status_after_gl219.reviewer_briefing: Developer Preview baseline only; not production-grade identity

runtime_infrastructure_status_after_gl22.postgresql_optional: validated with synthetic/demo data in ephemeral mode only

workspace_status_after_gl221.reviewer_briefing: workspace enforcement is a known gap — explicitly NOT production-complete

known_limitations.limitation: No production database RLS or row-level isolation
Workspace propagation for audit/evidence/provenance not complete

known_full_suite_false_positive_classes.description: Tests checking that branch-only changes are absent from main; trigger on branches introducing new docs/scripts
Tests scanning for forbidden file patterns that match legitimate test or script names
Tests checking for stale readiness language that match updated documentation wording
Tests asserting a specific migration count that becomes stale when new migrations are added
Errors from the publish-worktree path that pre-date GL-214 and are unresolved pre-existing issues
Failures from unrelated website-design/ import test scope that existed before GL-214
Errors triggered by missing GRANTLAYER_* environment variables in bare test runs
Branch-only scope-guard false positives for GL-222 docs/artifacts/scripts/tests added in this issue

security_sensitive_reporting_instruction.route_to: GitHub Security Advisories (private security disclosure mechanism)

checks: Forbidden file patterns absent (public export dirs, snapshot worktrees, setup.py, SDK pyproject.toml, package.json, deployment/cloud/Kubernetes files, TLS cert/key files, GitHub workflow changes for GL-222)
GL-222 docs/artifact content scanned for obvious secret-like text patterns (redacted in output)
Output explicitly states: not approval to publish, not a full security audit
does_not: Copy files to a public snapshot

production_readiness_impact: None. GL-222 is documentation and tooling only. All NO-GO decisions from GL-221 remain in force.
controlled_preview_impact: Confirms Controlled External Technical Review posture remains GO with strict boundaries per GL-221. Provides internal handoff documentation for a future review. Does not change controlled preview scope or synthetic/demo-only data boundary.

remaining_blockers.mitigation: Keep Developer Preview / Controlled Preview only
remaining_blockers.remaining_work: Complete production IAM, workspace, DB, ops, compliance gates
Data governance, legal/security approval, production controls
workspace identity, membership, ownership, query/mutation enforcement

remaining_blockers.description: No OIDC/SAML/SSO/MFA; static admin token only
remaining_blockers.mitigation: Token is dev-only; never in production

remaining_blockers.description: No production database or cloud infrastructure
Workspace propagation in audit/evidence not complete

remaining_blockers.remaining_work: Add workspace ownership predicates and tests

risk_register.risk: Static admin token in production context
risk_register.mitigation: Token is dev-only; never in production
GL-222 prohibited materials list; pre-handoff export scan required

Reviewer briefing before any actual review

findings: Controlled External Technical Review is GO with strict boundaries per GL-221 final readiness matrix v6
All ten required handoff questions are answered in this pack
No backend source changes were made in GL-222
No migrations, schema, dependency, GitHub workflow, or publish script changes were made
No public export, publish, or reviewer outreach was initiated
Unrelated website-design/import files are excluded from GL-222
The GL-222 handoff gate script is local-only, dry-run/plan-only, and non-destructive
All NO-GO decisions from GL-221 remain unchanged
